steps.title: Identify the grammatical need
steps.content: The sentence has "Scientists" as its subject but no main verb yet — the blank must supply a finite (tensed) verb that governs the that-clause beginning with "that the secret..."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘explain’: A finite present-tense verb that agrees with the plural subject "Scientists" and correctly governs the that-clause. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘having explained’: A nonfinite perfect participle; it cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause, violating the finite verb rule. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘explaining’: A nonfinite present participle; it cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause, violating the finite verb rule. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘to explain’: A nonfinite to-infinitive; it cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause, violating the finite verb rule. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
